headline: This DIY bakery allows you to make your own cake, pastries  GMA News Online
text:
Do you want to try your hand at baking, but you don't know how to start? A DIY bakery in Metro Manila allows you to make your own cake, perfect for beginners who wish for some guidance in the kitchen!
At the I Did It Myself (IDIM) DIY Bakery, all you need to bring is yourself and your creative mind to bake something nice for your loved ones.
You don't have to worry about the baking materials and ingredients you have to bring, as they have it all. All you have to do is follow the instructions for a smooth baking session. 
First, you will have to choose which cakes or pastries you want to try from the wide array of choices. The prices vary depending on what you choose to make.
Then, you will be settling down in your baking station and you will be given a tablet where all the instructions can be found. The instructions are precisely written, step by step, with a video demonstrating it for a better understanding. 
You only have two hours to complete your cake, but no worries as you can always ask for help from their friendly coaches. 
After baking, you can also create your own beverage. You can choose between iced coffee or milk tea, and have it mixed your way. 
But more than the baking experience, the DIY bakery is also a unique palace where you can spend quality time with your loved ones.
You can visit IDIM DIY Bakery at their three branches: 4th floor of Mega Atrium in SM Megamall, Level 2 Annex in SM North Edsa, and 4th floor, Building A, Ayala Malls Manila Bay.
—MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: Two-time Emmy winner to host show promoting global Filipinos on GMA Pinoy TV  GMA News Online
text: Two-time Emmy Award winner Marc Anthony Nicolas is set to host “Make Your Marc”, a talk show on GMA Pinoy TV that will share the stories of Pinoys around the world.Aside from the opportunity to spread the stories of global Filipinos, Nicolas said that he was particularly excited for the show because of his mother’s influence.“I think my mom would be so very proud if she was here on earth but I know she’s even more prouder in heaven,” he said in an interview with GMA Pinoy TV.“I think she would be so excited for me to have my very own talk show because she instilled so much positivity in me, and honestly every time I tape [and] every time I film, every time I talk to another person, I feel that I bring a part of my mom with me on set,” he added.The West Hollywood-based producer said he intends to share Filipino heritage and tradition through his show, and it was apparent through his chosen team of Pinoys.“It’s become a platform where people can actually share their stories, and I 'm just so lucky and so blessed to have this team behind me. Working on this project it’s so nice to see people that look like me and talk like me and share that special bond and heritage and tradition, and that's what makes it feel so special because I have that connection not only with the trail blazers who are Filipino but also the crew as well that are Filipino as well,” Nicolas said.Executive Producer Chris Gordon said  the show hopes to inspire and represent Filipinos who have made their own mark all over the world.“I feel as though there are a lot of people who will be inspired by these trail blazers ' stories and how they've become, how they've made their mark on the world,” Gordon said.“I feel as though it’s undeniable for Hollywood to see all of us, you know, and because we have great stories We can't deny those stories. We're very creative with how we tell these stories that Hollywood has not been used to portraying or giving to everyone,” he added.He also shared that it was his first experience working on a team composed of mostly Filipinos or Filipino-Americans, which made his experience with the project unique from others he has done.“I've never been on a set or worked on a project where just about everybody is a person of color or a Filipino-American or Filipino, and the production team, Christian, and his crew are young and they're vibrant and they're fresh and they're hungry… and you can feel it,” Gordon said.Meanwhile, director Christian Reyes said that the project was a “collaborative thing” that worked due to the trust given to the team.—Jiselle Anne Casucian/RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Nocum, Clarito, Belga join forces as Rain or Shine arrests slump at expense of Phoenix  GMA News Online
text:
It took Rain or Shine a collective effort to finally break out of its four-game slump as it thumped Phoenix, 100-85, in the 2024 PBA Philippine Cup on Sunday at the Ynares Center Antipolo.
Rookie Adrian Nocum atoned for a three-point outing in their previous game against San Miguel, churning out a team-high 28 points this time around, built on five triples alongside seven rebounds, six assists, and two steals as the Elasto Painters improved to 1-4. 
The Fuel Masters, however, absorbed their third loss in four games. 
The former Mapua standout immediately caught fire with 17 points at the half before veteran big man Beau Belga dropped 12 of his total output in the third, where they saw their lead balloon to 78-66. The 6-foot-5 bruiser ended up with a double-double effort of 21 points and 11 rebounds on top of three assists. 
Then it was Clarito who issued the finishing kick, burying nine straight points to fuel an 11-3 closing run that gave Rain or Shine a 100-83 cushion in the final 2:14 of the fourth.  
Clarito tallied 19 markers, five boards, three dimes, and one block. 
Ricci Rivero paced Phoenix with 16 points, including five consecutive points early in the fourth that cut their deficit to a single digit at 71-80, while RJ Jazul chipped in 11 markers. 
Scores:
Rain or Shine 100 – Nocum 28, Belga 21, Clarito 19, Santillan 8, Caracut 6, Belo 5, Mamuyac 4, Borboran 4, Norwood 3, Ildefonso 2, Asistio 0, Demusis 0.
Phoenix 85 – Rivero 16, Jazul 11, Tuffin 10, Verano 10, Manganti 7, Mocon 6, Alejandro 6, Muyang 5, Daves 4, Salado 3, Camacho 2, Lalata 0.
Quarters: 22-29, 47-41, 78-66, 100-85.
—Bea Micaller/JKC, GMA Integrated News

headline: Who is Rose Hanbury What you need to know about her connection to Prince William and Kate Middleton  GMA News Online
text:
The British Royal Family is currently facing a controversy amid the public absence of Kate Middleton, the Princess of Wales following an abdominal surgery in January and the furor surrounding the edited family photo released on Mother's Day in the United Kingdom.
As speculations on Kate's health and the state of her marriage to Prince William, the Prince of Wales keep spreading online, another name has been included in the picture: Rose Hanbury, who is being linked to the heir to the British throne.
So who is Rose and how is she connected to the Prince and Princess of Wales? 
Rose, Marchioness of Cholmondeley 
According to British publication The Independent, Rose Hanbury is a member of the British aristocracy. She became the Marchioness of Cholmondeley after marrying David Rocksavage, the 7th Marquess of Cholmondeley.
The Marquess of Cholmondeley is a title in the peerage of the United Kingdom. It was created in 1815 for George Cholmondeley, 4th Earl of Cholmondeley.
Rose first met David, who is 23 years her senior, while on holiday in Italy in 2003. They announced their engagement six years later and got married in 2009. The couple is blessed with three children: Alexander, Oliver, and Iris.
Her family has close ties with the British Royal family 
Rose is the daughter of web designer Timothy Hanbury and fashion designer Emma Hanbury. Her link to the Royal family started from her grandmother, Lady Elizabeth Lambart, who was a well-known figure in the royal circle. Elizabeth was a bridesmaid in Queen Elizabeth II's 1947 wedding to Prince Philip, the Duke of Edinburgh.
A former model and researcher 
Before marrying the Marquess of Cholmondeley, Rose was a model under top modeling agency Storm, when she was 23. She once made headlines in 2005, after being photographed on holiday with her younger sister, Marina, and Tony Blair.
She also worked as a researcher for Conservative politician Michael Gove but left after giving birth to her twin sons.
Her family resides near William and Kate's old residence
The Marchioness of Cholmondeley lives at Houghton Hall in Norfolk with her husband and their three children. Their house is located three miles away from Anmer Hall, the former home to the Prince and Princess of Wales. Rose is also said to be friends with Kate.
Present on royal occasions
As someone close to the British royal family, Rose has attended numerous royal events at Buckingham Palace. In 2017, she was spotted at a state banquet at Buckingham Palace, where she was seated next to Prince Harry.
She also attended the coronation in May last year of King Charles III and Queen Camilla. Her son, Oliver, was a Page of Honor, while her husband was appointed Lord-in-Waiting to King Charles.
Rumored affair with Prince William
American TV host and writer Stephen Colbert mentioned Rose's name during the March 12 episode of his show "The Late Show With Stephen Colbert."
"The Kingdom has been all a flutter about the seeming disappearance of Kate Middleton," Colbert said. "Well now, internet sleuths are guessing that Kate's absence may be related to her husband, and the future King of England, William, having an affair."
Colbert went on to name-drop Rose as the woman at the center of the rumor mill. He said that speculation about Rose and William first came up in 2019, when Kate was pregnant with their third child, Prince Louis.
"According to tabloids back then, when Kate supposedly confronted him about it, he laughed it off, saying there was nothing to it," Colbert said, jokingly adding, "Always a good response when you're wife accuses you of cheating."
As of writing, neither William, Kate, nor Rose publicly addressed the speculation.
—Jade Veronique Yap/MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: Easterlies bring cloudy skies, rain showers over parts of PH  GMA News Online
text: The easterlies are affecting several parts of the Philippines, according to PAGASA’s daily weather forecast on Sunday afternoon.The country will be having partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers and thunderstorms due to the combined effects of the easterlies and localized thunderstorms.Possible flash floods and landslides in different areas in the country as a result of said weather conditions.The country may also experience light to moderate winds with slight to moderate coastal waters.Sunrise in Metro Manila will be at 6:02 a.m. on Monday.—Jiselle Anne Casucian/RF, GMA Integrated News

headline: Rayver Cruz issues apology over viral livestream with Julie Anne San Jose 'I was just being myself'  GMA News Online
text:
Rayver Cruz issued an apology shortly after clips of his livestream with Julie Anne San Jose went viral.
On X (formerly Twitter), a netizen posted three clips from a recent livestream of the couple where the two were answering questions from netizens about their performances and the preparations behind them.
In the video, the Kapuso actor could be seen in a jolly mood that distracted Julie while she was talking on the livestream. Some people were also offended about Rayver subtly tapping Julie's head at one point. 
In a tweet, Rayver apologized to those who were offended by his actions in the livestream.
"Sa mga na-offend sa kilos ko kanina sa livestream namin, sorry," he wrote. "But I was just being myself."
"Playful and happy talaga ako, lalo na when I'm with my love," he added. "She knows that, she knows me."
Sa mga na offend sa kilos ko kanina sa live stream namin, sorry. But I was just being myself. Playful and happy talaga ako. Lalo na when I'm with my love. She knows that, she knows me. I don't usually speak up pero ibang usapan na ang accusations. Una, hinding hindi ko kayang…
Rayver also said he doesn't usually react to bashers, but it's a different story when accusations are involved.
"Una, hinding-hindi ko kayang saktan si Julie," he said. "My mom raised me right."
"Hinawakan ko 'yung buhok niya na lagi kong ginagawa sa mga iba pa naming videos 'pag magkasama kami pero kung iba po ang dating sa ibang tao, wala na po ako magagawa d'un," he added.
Rayver also said he respects and loves Julie, as well as her family.
"Pangalawa, may respeto ako sa trabaho ko at sa mga katrabaho ko," he said.
"Hinding hindi ako gagawa ng makakasira d'un," he added. "At sana, matuto na rin tayo rumespeto sa iba at maging malawak ang pag-iisip."
In a separate post, the Asia's Limitless Star also defended Rayver.
"Minsan lang po ako sumagot sa mga ganto but sobra ka na po," Julie said. "Below the belt and harsh na po itong accusations, please stop."
Minsan lang po ako sumagot sa mga ganto but sobra ka na po. Below the belt and harsh na po itong accusations, please stop ????????
Julie Anne and Rayver sparked romance rumors in 2022. They publicly exchanged "I love yous" at the JulieVerse concert that same year.
The couple has also discussed marriage, with Rayver confident he will marry Julie. The singer and actress also revealed that she wants a simple wedding.
—Hermes Joy Tunac/MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: Ukraine drones attack refinery, target Moscow, disrupt power - Russia  GMA News Online
text:
Ukraine launched 35 drones against broad areas of Russia, sparking a brief fire at an oil refinery, targeting Moscow and disrupting electricity in border areas, Russia said early on Sunday, the final day of the country's presidential vote.
Moscow accuses Kyiv of election sabotage with its days of strikes on Russian infrastructure, one of the most sweeping air operations on Russian territory since President Vladimir Putin ordered the invasion of neighboring Ukraine two years ago.
Putin, all but certain to win re-election, has vowed to punish Ukraine for the attacks.
Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy thanked his military forces and intelligence for new "long-range capabilities" in his nightly video address on Saturday, without mentioning the reported intensified attacks by his nation's forces.
Reuters could not independently verify the reports.
The Russian defense ministry posted on the Telegram messaging app on Sunday that 17 drones were destroyed over the southern Russian region of Krasnodar, four over the Moscow region, and the rest over six other regions.
"The drones were neutralized, but a fire broke out as a result of the fall of one of the devices," the operational headquarters of the Krasnodar region in southern Russia said on Telegram.
The fire at the Slavyansk refinery in Krasnodar was extinguished and there were no casualties as a result of the fire, although preliminary information indicated one person had died of a heart attack, the administration said.
Roman Siniagovskyi, head of the Slavyansk administrative district, said on Telegram that refinery workers were evacuated and there was no threat to nearby populated areas from the incident.
The Slavyansk refinery, some 70 km (45 miles) north of Krasnodar, processes around 4.5 million metric tons of crude a year, producing fuel mainly for exports, but also selling some gasoline on the domestic market.
Moscow Mayor Sergei Sobyanin said there were no casualties or damage reported from the drones shot down over three districts near Moscow.
North of Moscow four drones were destroyed over the Yaroslavl region, with no damage or injuries reported, Governor Mikhail Yevrayev said on Telegram.
In the border region of Belgorod, an attack by four Ukraine-launched drones damaged electricity and gas lines in one village, said Governor Vyacheslav Gladkov.
Russia's defense ministry said Russian forces also shot down 12 rockets launched from Ukraine towards the Belgorod region on Sunday.
As Russia was facing attacks from Ukraine, Kyiv's military said on Sunday that Russian air attacks had damaged agricultural enterprises and destroyed several industrial buildings in the Black Sea port of Odesa overnight. — Reuters

headline: GMA Network chairman Felipe L. Gozon serves as guest speaker at Central United Methodist Church's 125th anniversary  GMA News Online
text:
GMA Network Chairman Felipe L. Gozon was the special guest speaker during the 125th-anniversary celebration of the Philippines' first Protestant church, the Central United Methodist Church (CUMC), in Ermita, Manila.
On Sunday, the CUMC congregation marked its 125th founding anniversary with a celebration and worship, with Gozon delivering a special anniversary message.
Gozon recounted the history of how the church played a vital role in his life as a protestant and his family. He also cited similarities between CUMC and GMA Network.
"We share with CUMC the basic doctrines of our Christian faith and the values of integrity and stewardship," Gozon said. "The first in our seven Core Values is: 'We Place God Above All.'"
"We have helped millions of Filipinos in need through our relief, construction of classrooms and bridges, bloodletting, give-a-gift, medical, alay sa kinabukasan to our school children projects, among others," he said.
Aside from Gozon, among the attendees during the anniversary celebration were Gozon's wife, Teresa Gozon, and their three children, Annette Gozon-Valdes, Philip Gozon, and Maritess Gozon-Viterbo with their respective spouses.
In an interview with GMA Integrated News, Gozon said he is "very proud and honored" to have been invited as a guest speaker for CUMC.
In 2022, Gozon received the honorary degree of Doctor of Laws, Honoris Causa, in the same Methodist church. The occasion marked the first time an individual from the private sector was awarded such an honor.
In December last year, Gozon stepped down as the CEO of GMA Network, but he remains chairman of the board and will continue to serve as an adviser to the company.
GMA Network Chairman Felipe L. Gozon arrives at the Central United Methodist Church in Manila to serve as a guest speaker in celebration of the church's 125th anniversary in the Philippines. | @gmanews @gmanewsbreaking pic.twitter.com/TTelXduONo
—MGP, GMA Integrated News

headline: With upsurge, PDEA warns vs. marijuana-flavored vapes  GMA News Online
text: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) has cautioned the public against the use of vaping products that contain marijuana oil, stressing that it is illegal and poses a health risk to users.In a statement over the weekend, PDEA said their recent anti-illegal drugs operations revealed that there is an upsurge of marijuana-laced electronic cigarettes in the Philippine market.On March 14, two alleged drug personalities were arrested in separate anti-drug operations in Taguig City, resulting in the seizure of cannabis oil and marijuana kush, along with assorted vaping devices, with an estimated value of P842,000.PDEA and the Bureau of Customs also intercepted last week 18 balikbayan boxes containing cannabis oil and marijuana kush hidden inside e-cigarettes worth P337 million at the Port Area, Manila.“The selling and smuggling of marijuana oil-cartridges indicate that there is a growing domestic demand for these products. Considering that the vaping culture is predominantly popular among the youth, PDEA is wary that these cannabis extracts can pass off as a legitimate vape aerosol in the market and sold to the younger patrons,” PDEA said.“PDEA warns the public not to patronize marijuana-laced e-cigarettes because of the health hazards involved, and most importantly the law explicitly prohibits it. The proliferation of these illicit items can also expose unknowing customers to be addicted to marijuana,” it added.The agency said it would strengthen its monitoring to detect physical and online vape shops selling cannabis flavored e-cigarettes and other related paraphernalia.PDEA also sought the support from concerned regulating bodies to come up with tighter measures against vape stores, as well as retailers and importers, to prevent people from using prohibited substances.—RF, GMA Integrated News
